title: Completion publisher and close-record promotion
goal: Make successful-run publication atomic from the operator's perspective so implementation commits, slice close records, final reports, and handoff SHA cannot be split across hidden integration-branch commits or stale main-branch truth.
acceptance: A successful run advertises one final handoff SHA/branch state that contains both worker implementation changes and daemon-owned publication changes such as closed slice JSON and final report artifacts; close/report truth is not stranded on an unadvertised integration-branch commit.

A regression test covers the R8 failure shape: after gate success and slice closure, handoff/final report evidence points at a commit that includes the close records instead of only the implementation commit.

If slice metadata needed for closure is absent or unwritable, Khazad-Doom records a structured incident naming the affected slice/path and preserves or blocks handoff readiness according to explicit policy; it must not silently drop close-record truth.
Successful-run publication is resume-safe and idempotent: resuming after publication does not duplicate close/report commits, lose final SHA truth, or re-close already published slices incorrectly.
Handoff and final-report exit states remain read-only summaries over daemon lifecycle state; this slice must not introduce remote push/PR mutation or a second source of workflow truth.
Existing fake-runner end-to-end, handoff, incident, and resume tests remain green under the full verification profile.
must_ask_if: The implementation would make roadmap docs, reports, or handoff JSON authoritative over slice JSON plus daemon/run state.
The implementation would auto-push, create PRs, or mutate remotes as part of default publication.
The implementation would require an operator replan/approval step for normal successful publication instead of fixing the daemon-owned promotion path.
The implementation would add new run or slice terminal statuses for publication rather than preserving existing lifecycle states with better evidence.
The implementation would weaken slice-close invariants, ignore close failures without structured incidents, or treat missing close metadata as invisible success.
The implementation would broadly split or rewrite workflow::manager outside the completion-publication seam without evidence from the architecture review.
